title: Betasso Preserve Mountain Bike Trail - Complete. Boulder, Colorado

transcript.text: Welcome to Boulder, Colorado's Batasso Preserve mountain bike loop on the front range of Colorado.
It's very accessible. You just go up Boulder Canyon and it's up in near Sugarloaf.
And here's where it is.
That road on the right there is Highway 119 and the green circle is the parking lot of Batasso.
So the red circle on the left is the south loop, the blue is the Benjamin connector, and the yellow loop on the right is the north loop.
So doing both loops together is fun. So let's check it out.
So here we are at the beginning of the trail. We go from the parking lot down to the trail to the switchback is the first section.
The second section on my video here is from that switchback down to the gully.
I really like this section because there's lots of trees and turns and rocks and that's technical.
Okay, now there's that gully. We go from there around the trees, a nice fun section, cross another gully, and then it climbs up a little bit.
And then that Strava section ends on the climb between these two trees.
So that's the end of that Strava downhill section.
And now we're going to go from there around the edge of that ridge through the trees there and then past another little gully to the turnoff to go up to the climb.
Here's the beginning of the climb back to the car or down to the Benjamin loop connector.
So there's where it starts.
You go out on the ridge and then through the trees across a deep gully where there's a nice bridge and then climb up to the connector.
So there's the end of the Benjamin loop connector and the beginning of the northern loop.
And so it starts up there and goes through the trees.
It's a nice downhill all the way from there.
It works well for fat guys like me on the 26-inch mountain bike wheels, I think.
And then as you go up, you're on the higher part of the trail.
So let's zoom in here and just have a little look at the actual trail itself.
Those are where the two trails connect at the end of the Benjamin loop connector.
It goes all the way down.
It's a nice single track.
And then right there, that's where the Strava connector link section downhill starts, right across the river there.
Two four-mile connector stairs sections start.
Check it out, bitches.
I'm first place.
Ha, ha.
First place out of 390 people.
So back up from Highway 119, you do all that stuff you already did.
And then you peel off towards the right and go on a higher trail up through the trees and the side of the mountain.
It's got some good climbs and it has a nice good downhill, too, through this area here.
It goes around the ridge and then that's why I switch back and then to the connector.
Okay, so this is the end of the northern loop and the beginning of the Benjamin connector.
So you start right there.
You see going down across that nice bridge at the bottom of that deep valley through the trees and the shade and everything.
And then you have to start climbing again right there to the connector to the climb.
At the beginning of the climb, there it is, the turnoff.
You go through the trees, past the switchback, through a pretty good grade, not too hard, across the gully.
And then it starts to get steep until the top.
This is the last section from the top of the climb up, down, and then up the slight grade of the nice dirt road down the fast climb to the car.
